analysis.problem: Insufficient progress in increasing access to quality education, with persistent inequalities in education outcomes across the world
analysis.root_cause: Inadequate investment in education infrastructure, lack of qualified teachers, and socio-economic disparities
leverage_points: Government policies and funding for education
International organizations such as UNESCO and OECD

proposed_actions.action: Increase funding for education programs and infrastructure development
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: 10% increase in education budget allocation within the next 2 years

proposed_actions.action: Implement teacher training and qualification programs
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: 25% increase in qualified teachers in underserved areas within the next 5 years

evidence: The world has made progress in increasing basic education, but also faces challenges in improving education quality and access
UNESCO strategy for youth and adult literacy (2020-2025) highlights the need for inclusive education and lifelong learning
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of PLANETA (as education is linked to environmental sustainability and resource management) and CHOVEK (as education is crucial for human development and dignity)
analysis.summary: The world is facing challenges in improving access to quality education, with persistent inequalities in education outcomes, and requires increased investment and international cooperation to address these issues. Government policies and funding for education are key leverage points to drive progress.

raw_items.title: Global education
raw_items.summary: Global education is a mental development program that seeks to improve global human development based on the understanding of global dynamics, through the various sectors of human development delivery. raw_items.title: Global Education - Our World in Data
raw_items.summary: Explore data and insights on education outcomes, access, quality, and inequalities across the world. Learn how the world has made progress in increasing basic education, but also faces challenges in improving learning and reducing gaps.

raw_items.title: Literacy
raw_items.summary: Literacy is the ability to read and write, and illiteracy is the inability to read and write.
